pages.description:
Building global knowledge of marine life for local action 

Building global knowledge of marine life for local action     

Our Vision

pages.html:
Building global knowledge of marine life for local action

Building global knowledge of marine life for local action

Our Vision

By 2030 and beyond, anyone, anywhere, will have access to information on marine species and ecosystems important to local fisheries, culture, health, and livelihoods. We will be able to diagnose how species are shifting with climate change and management interventions, to achieve a sustainable future for nature and people.

What We Do

Convene

stakeholders to chart the course together, implementing equitable management for long-term coordination and financing of global marine life observation and applications

Leverage

emerging innovation to democratize marine life knowledge, using ‘omics, acoustics, imaging, and AI, and a sequence-based Ocean Biocode of global marine species

Integrate

biodiversity into a global ocean observation system by establishing and promoting interoperable biodiversity standards and best practices, integrating with other disciplines

Apply

enhanced knowledge of marine life to co-develop solutions, informing conservation and sustainable use of marine life by trained stakeholders in every coastal nation
pages.markdown:
## Building global knowledge of marine life for local action

## Building global knowledge of marine life for local action

![](/images/ML2030_LogoColor300ppi.png)

![](/images/TriColorWave.png)

## Our Vision

By 2030 and beyond, anyone, anywhere, will have access to information on marine species and ecosystems important to local fisheries, culture, health, and livelihoods. We will be able to diagnose how species are shifting with climate change and management interventions, to achieve a sustainable future for nature and people.

![](/images/BlueBottomWave.png)

## What We Do

 __

## Convene

stakeholders to chart the course together, implementing equitable management for long-term coordination and financing of global marine life observation and applications

__

## Leverage

emerging innovation to democratize marine life knowledge, using ‘omics, acoustics, imaging, and AI, and a sequence-based Ocean Biocode of global marine species

__

## Integrate

biodiversity into a global ocean observation system by establishing and promoting interoperable biodiversity standards and best practices, integrating with other disciplines

__

## Apply

enhanced knowledge of marine life to co-develop solutions, informing conservation and sustainable use of marine life by trained stakeholders in every coastal nation

pages.title: About
pages.description:
People depend on biodiversity —the heart of healthy ecosystems… yet biodiversity is the missing link in marine observing 

Marine Life 2030 is a program endorsed by the United Nations Decade of Ocean Science for Sustainable Development (the Ocean Decade) to establish a globally coordinated system that delivers knowledge of ocean life to those who need
pages.html:
People depend on biodiversity —the heart of healthy ecosystems… yet biodiversity is the missing link in marine observing

Marine Life 2030 is a program endorsed by the United Nations Decade of Ocean Science for Sustainable Development (the Ocean Decade) to establish a globally coordinated system that delivers knowledge of ocean life to those who need it, promoting human well-being, sustainable development, and ocean conservation.

Want to learn more about ML2030? Check out this recording from the AIR Centre hosted “Marine Biodiversity Networking Fridays.” This session, MBON and the organization of a global community for the Ocean Decade: Priorities, benefits, and co-design strategies, engaged participants from 57 countries in the design of the Marine Life 2030 Ocean Decade program, including a request to contribute to the leadership of themes and tasks.

pages.markdown:
# _People depend on biodiversity —the heart of healthy ecosystems… yet biodiversity is the missing link in marine observing_

![](/images/ML2030_WebLogoWhite_500PXH-300x250.png)

Marine Life 2030 is a program endorsed by the United Nations Decade of Ocean Science for Sustainable Development (the Ocean Decade) to establish a globally coordinated system that delivers knowledge of ocean life to those who need it, promoting human well-being, sustainable development, and ocean conservation.

Want to learn more about ML2030? Check out this recording from the AIR Centre hosted “[Marine Biodiversity Networking Fridays.](https://www.aircentre.org/netfridays-marine-biodiversity-01/)” This session, MBON and the organization of a global community for the Ocean Decade: Priorities, benefits, and co-design strategies, engaged participants from 57 countries in the design of the Marine Life 2030 Ocean Decade program, including a request to contribute to the leadership of themes and tasks.

## Our Partners

The following projects are affiliated with Marine Life 2030 and endorsed by the UN Decade of Ocean Science for Sustainable Development (UN Ocean Decade). Please email [email protected] if you would like to be connected with the leads of any of these projects.

In addition to the affiliated projects listed below, the Marine Life 2030 Ocean Decade Programme values and seeks active engagement with other UN Ocean Decade Programmes.

If you are engaged in a UN Ocean Decade Programme and are interested in coordinating with Marine Life 2030, please email us at [email protected].

pages.title: Join Us
pages.description:
Marine Life 2030 is implemented utilizing a bottom-up approach which relies on volunteers from all around the world. A series of learning communities have been established, focused on:  

Best Practices and Interoperability 

Co-design and Stakeholder Engagement 

Capacity Development Citizen Science 

Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPS) 

External Communications and
pages.html:
Marine Life 2030 is implemented utilizing a bottom-up approach which relies on volunteers from all around the world. A series of learning communities have been established, focused on:

  • Best Practices and Interoperability

  • Co-design and Stakeholder Engagement

  • Capacity Development Citizen Science

  • Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPS)

  • External Communications and Outreach

  • Indigenous Knowledge

  • Best Practices and Interoperability

  • Co-design and Stakeholder Engagement

  • Capacity Development Citizen Science

  • Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPS)

  • External Communications and Outreach

  • Indigenous Knowledge

More information about the program is available here and here.

If you are interested in affiliation with Marine Life 2030, please seek endorsement by the UN Ocean Decade requesting such affiliation (visit the UN Ocean Decade website to learn more). Please also review the Principles and Criteria for Marine Life 2030-affiliated Projects and Partners here.

The Marine Biodiversity Observation Network (MBON) calls on nations, scientists, sponsors, and stakeholders to work together as a community of practice to support Marine Life 2030 to enable a dialogue, and coordination and cooperation between programmes. Please see this Dear Colleague Letter and join the MBON/Marine Life 2030 Community of Practice (GEO BON Members).

Email us at [email protected] with questions or for more information.

We encourage you to download the above graphic and use it in your working sessions related to Marine Life 2030. If you use it, please consider sharing it on social media using the hashtag #MarineLife2030!
pages.markdown:
[![](/images/final_MarineLife_2030_v2-1-e1671565154817.png)](https://marinelife2030.org/wp-content/uploads/2023/01/final_MarineLife_2030_v2.pdf)

![](/images/TriColorWave.png)

Marine Life 2030 is implemented utilizing a bottom-up approach which relies on volunteers from all around the world. A series of learning communities have been established, focused on:

  * __

Best Practices and Interoperability

  * __

Co-design and Stakeholder Engagement

  * __

Capacity Development Citizen Science

  * __

Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPS)

  * __

External Communications and Outreach

  * __

Indigenous Knowledge




  * __

Best Practices and Interoperability

  * __

Co-design and Stakeholder Engagement

  * __

Capacity Development Citizen Science

  * __

Early Career Ocean Professionals (ECOPS)

  * __

External Communications and Outreach

  * __

Indigenous Knowledge




More information about the program is available [here](https://drive.google.com/file/d/13CszfRpt91_kLflGsjyEJblCeLjTiA96/view) and [here](https://drive.google.com/file/d/1eRC0v1manpQoSgcKphfNycFIVg1GYCJz/view).

If you are interested in affiliation with Marine Life 2030, please seek endorsement by the UN Ocean Decade requesting such affiliation (visit the UN Ocean Decade [website](https://forum.oceandecade.org/page/take-action) to learn more). Please also review the Principles and Criteria for Marine Life 2030-affiliated Projects and Partners [here](https://marinelife2030.org/wp-content/uploads/2024/04/ML2030-Principles-Criteria-for-Projects-and-Partners.docx.pdf).

The Marine Biodiversity Observation Network (MBON) calls on nations, scientists, sponsors, and stakeholders to work together as a community of practice to support Marine Life 2030 to enable a dialogue, and coordination and cooperation between programmes. Please see this [Dear Colleague Letter](https://marinelife2030.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/11/Dear-Colleague-Letter.pdf) and join the MBON/Marine Life 2030 Community of Practice ([GEO BON Members](https://members.geobon.org/pages/index)).

Email us at [email protected] with questions or for more information.

_We encourage you to download the above graphic and use it in your working sessions related to Marine Life 2030. If you use it, please consider sharing it on social media using the hashtag #MarineLife2030!_

pages.html:
Marine Life 2030 Logo Usage Policy: The Marine Life 2030 logo is intended to be used by approved partners, for informational and promotional purposes in support of the programme. Please email [email protected] to request approval to use, and to receive high resolution versions of, the logo. Review the Logo Use & Style Guide (linked to the left) for appropriate usage guidelines. Unauthorized use of the logo is strongly discouraged; please notify [email protected] of any unauthorized usages of the logo.

# **UPCOMING EVENTS**

## Ocean Best Practices Workshop VIII

14-18 October 2024, Virtual | <https://www.oceanbestpractices.org/ocean-best-practices-systems/news-and-events/workshop/workshop-viii/>

## One Ocean Science Congress

4-6 June 2025, Nice, France | <https://one-ocean-science-2025.org/>

9-13 June 2025, Nice, France | <https://sdgs.un.org/conferences/ocean2025>

If you are aware of other conferences or events that are relevant to the Marine Life 2030 community, please email them to [[email protected]](/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#620f03100b0c070e0b04075052515222050f030b0e4c010d0f).

posts.title: Letters to the Sea
posts.description:
Children and youth are the demographic group that will be the most affected by climate change. Yet, they are often left out of environmental discussions at the international level.

Somos OceanoS, a Marine Life 2030-affiliated project, wants to change this. They are excited to invite you to be a part
posts.html:
Letters to the Sea

Katie Fillingham2024-08-08T14:09:41+00:00

Children and youth are the demographic group that will be the most affected by climate change. Yet, they are often left out of environmental discussions at the international level.

Somos OceanoS, a Marine Life 2030-affiliated project, wants to change this. They are excited to invite you to be a part of a global dialogue between youth and decision-makers in marine conservation through their Letters to the Sea initiative. Their goal is to collect 1,000,000 letters for an art exhibition at the 2025 UN Ocean Conference in Nice, France.

  • Who can participate? Youth aged 3-22 years old from around the world.
  • What to write? Express your love for the ocean, share why we should care for it, or write your personal experiences. Drawings and letters in any language are welcome!
  • Why participate? Your voice matters! Letters to the Sea have been shown around the world, including at the UN Ocean Decade Conference and World Water Forum. Your letter might be adopted and answered by global leaders, making a real impact on marine conservation.

For more information, and to submit a letter, please visit: https://linktr.ee/letterstothesea.
posts.markdown:
# Letters to the Sea

[Katie Fillingham](https://marinelife2030.org/author/kfillingham/ "Posts by Katie Fillingham")2024-08-08T14:09:41+00:00

![](/images/3.-socmed-letters-to-the-sea-invitation-v3-300x300.png)Children and youth are the demographic group that will be the most affected by climate change. Yet, they are often left out of environmental discussions at the international level.

Somos OceanoS, a Marine Life 2030-affiliated project, wants to change this. They are excited to invite you to be a part of a global dialogue between youth and decision-makers in marine conservation through their **Letters to the Sea** initiative. **Their goal is to collect 1,000,000 letters for an art exhibition at the 2025 UN Ocean Conference in Nice, France.**

  * **Who can participate?** Youth aged 3-22 years old from around the world.
  * **What to write?** Express your love for the ocean, share why we should care for it, or write your personal experiences. Drawings and letters in any language are welcome!
  * **Why participate?** Your voice matters! **Letters to the Sea** have been shown around the world, including at the UN Ocean Decade Conference and World Water Forum. Your letter might be adopted and answered by global leaders, making a real impact on marine conservation.



For more information, and to submit a letter, please visit: [https://linktr.ee/letterstothesea.](https://linktr.ee/letterstothesea)
